_meta.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
_meta.purpose: Formal specification of handshake mechanisms where EITT/CoDa can interface with domains beyond the nuclear stability boundary. Each handshake defines: what the composition IS, what the simplex looks like, what the log-ratio transform DOES in that domain, what σ²_A MEASURES physically, and what an EITT proof would require.
_meta.hivip_level: L0-L1 (proposed interfaces, not yet tested). Each handshake must earn its own promotion through domain-specific validation.
_meta.principle: The simplex is the same everywhere. EITT reads compositional change — it does not need to know what the components ARE. The geometry is universal; the interpretation is domain-specific.
_meta.governance: HUF-GOV: instrument reads, never actuates. Each handshake produces a diagnostic. Domain experts interpret.

the_boundary.what_it_is: The nuclear stability boundary where SEMF (nucleons as discrete objects) breaks down. Beyond Z≈363 (B/A < 0), conventional nuclear matter cannot exist. The compositional lens (SEMF 4-part simplex, σ²_A) confirms Z≈114-126 as the last island. Beyond that boundary lies quark matter, where the degrees of freedom change from nucleons to quarks.
the_boundary.why_it_matters: EITT reached this boundary by following the compositional signal from energy-mix monitoring (EMBER) through geochemistry (40,666 rocks) through nuclear binding (AME2020) to the point where the signal dissolves. The question: does it dissolve, or does it transform into a signal readable by the same geometry from the other side?
the_boundary.the_answer: It transforms. Every domain beyond the boundary has compositional data on a simplex. None of them use CoDa. The handshake is possible because the Aitchison geometry that works on energy carriers also works on quark flavors, hadron yields, and r-process abundances.

the_composition.what_they_are: Hadron species yields at chemical freeze-out. When the QGP cools and hadronizes, it freezes into a composition of particle species. The yield ratios (K/π, Λ/π, etc.) are currently fitted independently.
the_composition.why_its_compositional: Total yield sums to closure. Changing one species fraction necessarily changes others. The constraint is currently IGNORED in chi-squared fits. Aitchison geometry respects it naturally.
the_composition.data_form: Yield ratios at each collision energy √s_NN. Time series: scan across √s_NN from 3 GeV (BES-II low) to 5.02 TeV (LHC).

the_handshake.clr_transform: clr of hadron yield vector at each √s_NN. Moves from the simplex to Euclidean space where distances are meaningful.
the_handshake.sigma2_A_measures: COMPOSITIONAL STRESS of the hadronization. High σ²_A = one species dominates (pion-dominated at high √s_NN). Low σ²_A = balanced production (more strange particles emerging at lower √s_NN where strangeness enhancement peaks).
the_handshake.perturbation: Δ(√s) = C( yield(√s₂)/yield(√s₁) ) — the perturbation between two collision energies. This IS the EITT diagnostic applied to the energy scan.
the_handshake.aitchison_distance: d_A between freeze-out compositions at adjacent energies. Spike = phase transition signature. The QCD critical point would appear as a drift flag.
the_handshake.eitt_decimation: Geometric mean of freeze-out compositions across energy bins. If Shannon entropy is invariant under decimation (EITT holds), then the freeze-out composition has the same structure at coarse and fine energy resolution. If it BREAKS, the breaking point marks the phase transition.
the_handshake.drift_flag: d_A(√s → √s+δ) > μ + 2σ flags the energy where the hadronic composition changes abruptly. This is a model-independent QCD critical point detector.

proof_of_concept.data_needed: Published STAR BES-II particle yields (π±, K±, p, p̄, Λ, Ξ, Ω) at each of ~10 energy points
proof_of_concept.test: Compute d_A between adjacent energies. Plot d_A vs √s_NN. Look for drift flag. Compare to known crossover temperature (T_c ≈ 155 MeV).
proof_of_concept.prediction: d_A should show structure (not flat) across the BES energy range. If a critical point exists, d_A should spike.
proof_of_concept.sample_size: ~10 energy points × 7-12 species = feasible with published data
proof_of_concept.estimated_effort: 1-2 days for a motivated physicist with CoDa background

HS-01.what_they_gain: Model-independent compositional drift detection. Currently the SHM extracts T and μ_B by fitting ratios independently — this loses the simplex constraint. CoDa preserves it. The d_A drift flag is parameter-free.

HS-02.domain: QCD Phase Diagram — Information Geometry Bridge
key_researchers: Authors of PRD 109 096017 (2024 FRG thermodynamic geometry on QCD)

the_composition.components: At each (T, μ_B) point: fractions of hadronic vs partonic degrees of freedom, or equivalently the quark condensate ⟨q̄q⟩ and Polyakov loop ⟨L⟩ as order parameters
the_composition.why_its_compositional: The QCD crossover is a continuous change in composition from hadronic to partonic matter. The order parameters trace a path through a state space that has simplex-like structure when normalized.

the_handshake.mathematical_bridge: Aitchison geometry on the D-simplex is isometric to ℝ^(D-1) with the standard Euclidean metric via the ILR transform. The Fisher-Rao metric on the multinomial family, restricted to the simplex, is conformally related to the Aitchison metric. Ruppeiner's thermodynamic metric IS the Fisher-Rao metric applied to thermodynamic fluctuations. Therefore: Aitchison ↔ Fisher-Rao ↔ Ruppeiner. The bridge is exact, not analogical.
the_handshake.sigma2_A_measures: Scalar curvature of the compositional manifold. In Ruppeiner geometry, curvature divergence = phase transition. In Aitchison geometry, σ²_A extremum = compositional stress change. These are measuring the same thing in different coordinates.
the_handshake.eitt_contribution: EITT adds TIME. Ruppeiner geometry is static (equilibrium). EITT applies to time-evolving compositions. The QGP evolves from initial state through thermalization to freeze-out — that's a time series on the simplex. EITT can read entropy invariance along this evolution.
the_handshake.the_union: Ruppeiner curvature detects WHERE the phase transition is in (T, μ_B). EITT detects HOW the composition changes across it. Together: geometry + dynamics.

proof_of_concept.data_needed: Lattice QCD equation of state at μ_B = 0 (publicly available from HotQCD collaboration). Susceptibilities χ_2, χ_4 as function of T.
proof_of_concept.test: Construct composition vector from partial pressures (hadronic, gluonic, quark contributions) as function of T. Compute σ²_A(T). Look for extremum near T_c ≈ 155 MeV.
proof_of_concept.prediction: σ²_A should show a minimum or inflection near T_c — the composition is maximally balanced at the crossover.
proof_of_concept.estimated_effort: 2-3 days, requires familiarity with lattice QCD data formats

HS-03.domain: Quark Flavor Simplex — udQM Continent of Stability

the_composition.what_they_are: Quark flavor fractions in bulk quark matter. The simplex is already 2D (ternary diagram). Nuclear matter sits near (u≈1/3, d≈2/3, s≈0). Strange quark matter sits near (u≈1/3, d≈1/3, s≈1/3). udQM sits at (u, d, s≈0) with specific u/d ratio.
the_composition.why_its_compositional: Quark number conservation enforces closure. The three flavors sum to total baryon number. The ground state of quark matter is literally a point on a 2-simplex.

the_handshake.clr_transform: clr(u, d, s) maps the ternary diagram to ℝ². In CLR coordinates, nuclear matter, udQM, and SQM occupy distinct regions.
the_handshake.sigma2_A_measures: FLAVOR BALANCE. Nuclear matter has high σ²_A (dominated by d quarks — 2 down per neutron, 1 up). SQM has low σ²_A (all three flavors equal). udQM has intermediate σ²_A (s=0 but u/d can balance).
the_handshake.aitchison_distance: d_A from nuclear matter to udQM to SQM measures the compositional distance of the phase transition. The Bodmer-Witten question (is SQM the ground state?) is: is d_A(nuclear → SQM) energetically downhill?
the_handshake.perturbation_path: The transition nuclear → udQM → SQM can be parameterized as a path on the simplex. EITT can test: is the path entropy-invariant? If yes, the transition preserves compositional structure. If no, where does it break?
the_handshake.the_insight: The continent of stability (A > 300) begins exactly where the SEMF compositional structure dissolves (Z > 363, B/A < 0). One simplex ends. Another begins. The handshake IS the boundary.

proof_of_concept.data_needed: Holdom-Ren-Zhang energy functional. Quark flavor fractions as function of baryon number A from their 2018 PRL model.
proof_of_concept.test: Compute σ²_A of the (u, d, s) composition as function of A across the transition from nuclear to quark matter. Look for the σ²_A minimum — compositionally calmest quark matter.
proof_of_concept.prediction: σ²_A should have a minimum at the SQM point (1/3, 1/3, 1/3) and a saddle near the nuclear-udQM boundary.
proof_of_concept.estimated_effort: 1 day. The simplex is only 2D. Visualization is immediate.

HS-04.domain: Neutron Star Interior — Radial Composition Profile

the_composition.extension: In the core: add hyperons (Λ, Σ, Ξ) for D=7, or quarks (u, d, s) for D=7 total
the_composition.parameterized_by: Baryon density n_B (from 0 at surface to ~5-10 n_0 at center, where n_0 = 0.16 fm⁻³ = nuclear saturation density)
the_composition.why_its_compositional: Charge neutrality + beta equilibrium constrain the composition at each density. The proton fraction Y_p as function of n_B traces a 1D path through the 4-simplex.

the_handshake.clr_transform: clr(n, p, e, μ) at each density shell. The radial profile of a neutron star IS a compositional time series — density plays the role of time.
the_handshake.sigma2_A_measures: COMPOSITIONAL UNIFORMITY of the matter at each density. Low σ²_A = balanced composition (matter has diverse particle content). High σ²_A = neutron-dominated (nearly pure neutron matter). The crust-core boundary, nuclear pasta phases, and hadron-quark transition should all show σ²_A structure.
the_handshake.perturbation: Δ(n_B) = C( x(n_B + δ) / x(n_B) ) — the perturbation between adjacent density shells. This is EITT applied to the radial coordinate.
the_handshake.eitt_decimation: Coarse-grain the density profile (average over shells). If EITT holds, Shannon entropy of the composition is invariant under this decimation. If it breaks, the breaking point marks a phase transition inside the star.
the_handshake.drift_flag: d_A spike between adjacent density shells = phase boundary. The crust-core boundary, the nuclear pasta transitions, and the possible hadron-quark transition should all produce drift flags.
the_handshake.the_union: The neutron star is a compositional gradient from vacuum (surface) to the densest matter in the universe (core). EITT reads the entire gradient as a single compositional time series. Every phase transition is a drift flag.

proof_of_concept.data_needed: Any published EOS table (e.g., APR, SLy4, or the Annala et al. 2020 posterior). These give particle fractions as function of n_B.
proof_of_concept.test: Compute d_A(n_B → n_B + δ) along the EOS. Plot d_A vs n_B. Look for drift flags at known phase boundaries.
proof_of_concept.prediction: Drift flags at n_B ≈ 0.5 n_0 (crust-core), n_B ≈ 2 n_0 (possible hyperon onset), n_B ≈ 4-5 n_0 (possible quark deconfinement).
proof_of_concept.connection_to_observation: Neutron star mass-radius measurements (NICER) and gravitational wave tidal deformability (LIGO/Virgo) constrain the EOS. The σ²_A profile provides a new observable that could discriminate between competing EOS models.
proof_of_concept.estimated_effort: 2-3 days. EOS tables are freely available. The computation is straightforward.

the_composition.components: Elemental mass fractions X_Z for Z=1..92+ (or binned by atomic mass regions: light, first-peak, second-peak, rare-earth, third-peak, actinides)
the_composition.what_they_are: The final elemental abundance pattern after r-process nucleosynthesis in neutron star merger ejecta. Different ejecta components (dynamical, disk wind, neutrino-driven) produce different compositions.
the_composition.parameterized_by: Electron fraction Y_e of the ejecta, which controls the neutron richness and hence the r-process path

the_handshake.clr_transform: clr of the abundance pattern. In CLR space, different Y_e trajectories trace different paths through compositional space.
the_handshake.sigma2_A_measures: ELEMENTAL DIVERSITY of the ejecta. Low σ²_A = broad, balanced abundance pattern (many elements produced in similar amounts — high metallicity, actinide-rich). High σ²_A = dominated by a few elements (iron-peak or light elements).
the_handshake.perturbation: Δ(Y_e) = C( X(Y_e + δ) / X(Y_e) ) — how the abundance pattern changes as electron fraction shifts. This maps the sensitivity of nucleosynthesis to initial conditions.
the_handshake.eitt_decimation: Coarse-grain the abundance pattern (bin adjacent elements). If EITT holds, the Shannon entropy of the abundance composition is invariant under binning. This would mean the r-process has a scale-invariant compositional structure — a profound claim about nucleosynthesis.
the_handshake.drift_flag: d_A between abundance patterns at adjacent Y_e values. Spikes mark where the r-process path jumps to a qualitatively different trajectory (e.g., crossing the N=82 or N=126 neutron shell closures).
the_handshake.the_union: The r-process builds elements by capturing neutrons — it traverses the SAME valley of stability that EITT mapped with SEMF. The nuclear staircase (dN/2 step code) determines WHERE the r-process waits (at magic numbers) and what it builds. The calm channel predicted by σ²_A should correlate with r-process abundance peaks.

proof_of_concept.data_needed: Published r-process yield tables from any major simulation (e.g., Rosswog, Korobkin, or Lippuner & Roberts SkyNet code). Yields as function of Y_e.
proof_of_concept.test: Compute d_A between abundance patterns at different Y_e. Plot d_A vs Y_e. Look for drift flags near Y_e ≈ 0.25 (transition from heavy to light r-process) and Y_e ≈ 0.40 (transition from r-process to alpha-process).
proof_of_concept.prediction: r-process abundance peaks (A≈80, 130, 195) should correspond to σ²_A calm zones in the nuclear staircase analysis. The step-code regime boundaries should predict WHERE abundance peaks form.

HS-06.contact_point: The boundary where SEMF composition ends and quark flavor composition begins
key_researchers: Combined: HS-01 through HS-05 researchers + Holdom group

the_composition.SEMF_side: [Volume, Surface, Coulomb, Asymmetry] — D=4 on the nuclear simplex
the_composition.quark_side: [u, d, s] — D=3 on the flavor simplex
the_composition.the_boundary_question: Can the two simplices be JOINED at the transition? Is there a D=7 (or reduced) joint simplex that spans both regimes?

the_handshake.approach: At the transition density (n_B ≈ 2-5 n_0), both descriptions coexist. The SEMF composition describes the hadronic fraction; the quark flavor composition describes the deconfined fraction. A mixed-phase model would have a composition like [Volume_had, Surface_had, Coulomb_had, Asymmetry_had, u_quark, d_quark, s_quark] on a 6-simplex.
the_handshake.sigma2_A_measures: TOTAL compositional stress across both phases simultaneously. At the transition, the joint composition should show structure — either a minimum (smooth crossover) or a discontinuity (first-order transition).
the_handshake.eitt_test: If EITT holds across the boundary (Shannon entropy invariant under geometric-mean decimation of the joint composition), then the two phases share a common compositional structure. If EITT breaks AT the boundary, the break itself characterizes the nature of the phase transition.
the_handshake.prediction: The EITT breaking point (if any) at the nuclear-quark boundary should coincide with the QCD critical point. This gives EITT a falsifiable prediction in QCD.
the_handshake.the_union: This is the ultimate HUF-GOV union. One tool reading two domains through a single geometry. The simplex is the same everywhere — the components change, the geometry doesn't. If the handshake succeeds, EITT spans from energy-mix monitoring through geochemistry through nuclear binding through the quark-hadron transition. One tool, one geometry, five domains.

proof_of_concept.approach: Use a hybrid EOS (e.g., the Annala et al. construction that smoothly interpolates between nuclear and quark matter). Extract the composition at each density. Build the joint simplex. Compute σ²_A across the transition.
proof_of_concept.estimated_effort: 1-2 weeks. Requires careful handling of the two simplex spaces and their union.
proof_of_concept.significance: If this works, it's a paper. A CoDa-geometric characterization of the QCD phase transition that connects to nuclear structure, astrophysics, and heavy-ion experiments simultaneously.

immediate.HS-01: QGP freeze-out. Data exists (STAR BES-II published yields). Computation is straightforward. Result is model-independent. Could be done in 1-2 days. HIGHEST IMPACT for lowest effort.
immediate.HS-03: Quark flavor simplex. Only 2D. Visualization is immediate. Connects directly to the continent-of-stability literature.

near_term.HS-04: Neutron star EOS. EOS tables are freely available. The computation is clean. Connects to NICER/LIGO observational programme.
near_term.HS-05: r-Process abundances. Direct connection to the nuclear staircase already mapped.

ambitious.HS-02: QCD information geometry bridge. Requires lattice QCD data handling. But the mathematical bridge (Aitchison ↔ Fisher-Rao ↔ Ruppeiner) is exact and publishable.
ambitious.HS-06: Joint nuclear-quark simplex. The ultimate test. Requires hybrid EOS expertise. If it works, it unifies the programme.

what_eitt_brings_to_the_table.model_independence: EITT reads compositional change without assuming a model. Chi-squared fits assume thermal equilibrium. CoDa doesn't.
what_eitt_brings_to_the_table.simplex_constraint: All these domains have compositions summing to closure. Current methods IGNORE this constraint. CoDa respects it. This isn't a convenience — it's a correction. Treating ratio data as unconstrained violates the mathematical structure of the data.
what_eitt_brings_to_the_table.entropy_invariance_test: EITT's core claim — Shannon entropy is near-invariant under geometric-mean decimation — is testable in EVERY domain. Each handshake produces a new EITT proof or a new failure mode. Both are scientifically valuable.
what_eitt_brings_to_the_table.drift_detection: The d_A drift flag is a universal phase-transition detector. It works on energy-mix data (EMBER). It works on geochemistry (40,666 rocks). If it works on QGP freeze-out data, the chain is: energy monitoring → geochemistry → nuclear binding → QCD. One tool across four boundaries.
what_eitt_brings_to_the_table.the_proven_chain: EMBER (energy) → Gold/Silver (finance) → U-235 (nuclear) → Ball 2022 (geochemistry) → NGFS (scenarios) → Nuclear staircase (AME2020). Six domains, six proofs. The next handshake extends the chain across the nuclear boundary.

what_we_do_NOT_claim: EITT discovers new physics (it reads existing structure through a compositional lens)
CoDa replaces domain expertise in QCD, nuclear astrophysics, or particle physics
The handshake mechanisms work before they are tested — each must earn promotion independently
The mathematical bridge (Aitchison ↔ Fisher-Rao) has been proven in the physics context — it is exact mathematically but unverified in physical application
Any handshake supersedes the native methods of the receiving domain

governance.hivip_level: L0 (proposed handshake) until domain-specific validation promotes each
governance.contamination_doctrine: Each handshake must be validated INDEPENDENTLY. Success in HS-01 does not promote HS-02. The anti-corruption rule applies: no handshake climbs by borrowing credibility from another.
governance.open_loop: The diagnostics produced by each handshake are open to interpretation by domain experts. EITT reads; humans decide.
governance.falsifiable: Each handshake includes a specific prediction. If the prediction fails, the handshake fails honestly and we learn something.
